article_title: Rogo Closes $160 Million Series D Led by Kleiner Perkins as Wall Street Embraces Agentic AI

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Rogo Closes $160 Million Series D Led by Kleiner Perkins as Wall Street Embraces Agentic AI
content_preview.summary: The New York AI startup, used by 35,000 finance professionals at firms like Rothschild and Jefferies, brought total funding past $300 million to scale Felix, its autonomous deal-workflow agent.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Rogo, an artificial intelligence platform built for financial services, said on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, that it has raised $160 million in a Series D round led by Kleiner Perkins, [according to a company announcement distributed by PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/rogo-raises-160m-series-d-to-scale-the-agentic-platform-for-finance-302756546.html). The deal brings Rogo's total funding to more than $300 million and lands as venture capital concentrates around A...

editor_notes.content_quality: Tight News piece, 562 words. Disciplined investor-list reproduction; conservative on valuation (deliberately omitted because the cited sources don't disclose it).

source_verification.source-0_prnewswire: Verified verbatim: $160M Series D, Kleiner Perkins lead, full investor syndicate (Sequoia, Thrive Capital, Khosla Ventures, J.P. Morgan Growth Equity Partners, BoxGroup, Mantis VC, Jack Altman, Evantic, Positive Sum), $300M+ total funding, 35,000+ professionals at 250+ institutions including Rothschild & Co + Jefferies + Lazard + Moelis + Nomura, Felix description 'executes complex, multi-step financial processes autonomously, from deal screening and CIM generation to buyer outreach and data room diligence', Stengel 'AI-native firms, with agentic systems' quote, Hamid 'combination of technical depth, proprietary data integrations, and genuine domain expertise' quote.
source_verification.source-1_pymnts: Snapshot served via Archive.org (April 30 capture). Verified: cofounders Gabe Stengel + John Willett, Willett 'former JPMorgan Chase banker', Stengel quote 'Finance runs on judgment, relationships, and insight... assembling decks and rebuilding models instead of talking to clients'.
source_verification.source-2_siliconangle: Verified: April 29 2026, Felix email-interaction model, automatic refresh-on-earnings flow.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim. The agent's note about deliberately not citing the unverified $2B valuation, BofA/Wells Fargo/JPM/GIC client list (which appears in PYMNTS but not the official press release), or the $75M Series C historic context (Rogo blog only) — these are exactly the right judgment calls for a press-release-grade story.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and disciplined. The 'What We Don't Know' section is honest about the valuation and ARR omissions and the Felix audit-trail/HITL gap.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Rogo archive coverage. Fresh story.
concerns: PYMNTS source-1 served via Archive.org fallback (not bot-blocked but the live URL evidently triggered the archive path). Snapshot is dated April 30 2026 and captures the original PYMNTS article cleanly.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Cryptography, sources, content, and quoting discipline all clean. Notable for the conservative omissions of unverified figures.
